title: The Most Underrated Golf State in America
body:
### I've played two of Wisconsin's best courses. Now I can't stop thinking about going back.

If you asked a hundred golfers to name the best golf states in America, Wisconsin might not crack most lists. 

You would hear about California. You would hear about Florida, the Carolinas, Arizona, and Oregon. You might even get a few people throwing out Michigan or Nebraska.

Wisconsin? Maybe not.

That is exactly why I am writing this.

I have played two of the best courses Wisconsin has to offer. Both of them left a mark. And somewhere between the Flower Hole at SentryWorld and the back nine at Erin Hills, I became completely convinced that Wisconsin is the most slept-on golf state in the country.

The problem now is that I have a list of courses I still need to play, and it is getting harder to ignore.


## WHAT I HAVE PLAYED
### Two Courses That Changed My Mind

SentryWorld in Stevens Point is where it started for me.
![SentryWorld - Hole 16  (Credit: SentryWorld website)](https://res.cloudinary.com/dak67vton/image/upload/v1777008635/a078de99-8033-412b-b337-52c36d508aac.png)
*SentryWorld - Hole 16  (Credit: SentryWorld website)*

If you have followed CYNTP for any amount of time you already know about the Flower Hole and golfing at SentryWorld. But what people do not talk about enough is the overall experience. The conditions are flawless every single day, maintained to Augusta National standards using the same SubAir system beneath every green. The variety of the layout keeps you engaged from start to finish. And the $375 green fee includes unlimited food and drinks for the entire round. Snacks, ice cream, sodas, alcoholic beverages, all covered. It ranks 49th on Golf Digest's list of America's 100 Greatest Public Courses and it is unlike anything else in the country. 

****Erin Hills is a completely different experience and equally unforgettable.****
![Erin hills](https://res.cloudinary.com/dak67vton/image/upload/v1777009303/Screenshot_2026-04-24_at_12.41.37_AM_zlyh4j.png)
*Erin Hills Golf Course - (Credit Official Erin Hills Website)*


The moment you walk onto the property, you know you are somewhere special. You feel it before you even get to the first tee. The whole place has an energy to it. A sense that something significant is about to happen.

Erin Hills is built in the spirit of the classic courses of Ireland and Scotland. The designers took their cues directly from those links traditions, routing 652 acres of rolling Wisconsin terrain sculpted by glaciers 10,000 years ago into a course that plays firm and fast with native fescue grasses swaying in the wind. The lodge is built in the style of an 18th-century Irish manor home. There is a pub on site. The whole experience feels transported.

This is a serious golf course. It hosted the 2017 U.S. Open and plays through the glacial kettles and ridges of the Kettle Moraine region in a way that demands your full attention from the first tee to the last green. The property is enormous and you feel that scale all day.

We walked it with caddies. No carts. That is the only way to play Erin Hills and it made the whole thing feel like a different game. The caddies know every slope, every wind, every trick that course has. By the back nine you feel like you are actually part of the place rather than just a visitor moving through it. It is the true way to play golf and Erin Hills earns every step.

Two very different courses. Both excellent. Both in a state that most golfers overlook entirely.


## WHAT I STILL NEED TO PLAY
### The List That Keeps Me Up at Night
Here is the honest problem with discovering Wisconsin golf. Once you start looking, you cannot stop finding reasons to go back.

****Sand Valley Resort in Nekoosa is at the top of my list and it is not close.****
![Sand Valley](https://res.cloudinary.com/dak67vton/image/upload/v1777009634/2e92f901-8951-4d92-93bc-fe726afd0fdc.png)
*Sand Valley - (Credit: Golf.com)*


This place is the Bandon Dunes of the Midwest. Built on 12,000 acres of natural duneland in central Wisconsin, Sand Valley is home to multiple world-class golf courses with more on the way. The original Sand Valley course, designed by Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw, ranks among the top public courses in America. Mammoth Dunes, the resort's second full course, ranks right alongside it. And The Lido, a stunning recreation of the legendary Golden Age course that was lost for decades, now ranks among the top courses in the entire world.

All of this is on one property. In Wisconsin.

Sand Valley is walking only. No carts. The resort follows the same philosophy as Bandon Dunes, golf first and everything else second. It sits about three hours from Chicago and Minneapolis, which makes it genuinely accessible for a long weekend trip.

I need to go. Soon.

****Whistling Straits in Kohler is probably the most famous course in Wisconsin and still one that most average golfers have never actually played.****

![Whistling Straits](https://res.cloudinary.com/dak67vton/image/upload/v1777009763/0ee686d9-7181-4d09-9f8d-a987fd6dec86.png)
*Whistling Straits - (Credit PGA.com)*

You have seen it on television. The 2021 Ryder Cup. Three PGA Championships. Over a thousand bunkers scattered across a Pete Dye design that runs along two miles of Lake Michigan shoreline. Owner Herb Kohler told Dye he wanted the course to look like Ireland, and Dye delivered something that feels genuinely transported from another place and time.

It is open to the public. Peak season rates run around $485 to $555 for the Straits Course depending on the time of year. Add a caddie fee of $90 plus gratuity and you are looking at a real investment for one round. But for a course that has hosted some of the biggest events in golf and sits directly on Lake Michigan, the bucket list case is impossible to argue with.

To secure a tee time on the Straits Course you generally need to book lodging at a Destination Kohler property. It is a resort experience more than a walk-up tee time. Plan accordingly and book as early as possible.

The Irish Course right next door is a more accessible option with peak rates around $270 and ranks among the top public courses in the country on its own.


## WHAT IT COSTS
### A Real Look at the Numbers

Here is a breakdown of what each course runs so you can plan accordingly. Note that this is the best I could find that is available online without calling each of these properties for more information (you know how hard it can be to find true rates sometimes). 

****SentryWorld:**** $375 per round. And before you react to that number, here is what it includes. Food and drinks for the entire round. All of it. Snacks, ice cream, sodas, alcoholic drinks, whatever you want. Covered from the first tee to the last green. When you factor that in, $375 for a top-50 public course in America with an all-inclusive experience is genuinely one of the best value propositions in the country. There is nothing else like it. [View rates and book SentryWorld](https://www.sentryworld.com/golf/tee-times-and-rates?utm_campaign=the-most-underrated-golf-state-in-america&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=courses-newsletter-56d38d.beehiiv.com)

****Erin Hills:**** Peak season rate (May 26 through September 27) is $495 per round. Shoulder season (early May and late September through mid-October) drops to $395. Caddie fee is $65 per player with a suggested gratuity of $75 or more. Erin Hills is walking only. [View rates and book Erin Hills](https://erinhills.com/golf/rates-fees/?utm_campaign=the-most-underrated-golf-state-in-america&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=courses-newsletter-56d38d.beehiiv.com)

****Sand Valley Resort:**** Rates vary by course and season. Check the booking page directly for current pricing across Sand Valley, Mammoth Dunes, The Lido, and Sedge Valley. All courses are walking only. [View rates and book Sand Valley](https://www.sandvalley.com/golf-courses/rates?utm_campaign=the-most-underrated-golf-state-in-america&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=courses-newsletter-56d38d.beehiiv.com)

****Whistling Straits:**** Peak season rates for the Straits Course run approximately $485 to $555. The Irish Course runs around $270 at peak. Caddie fee is $90 per player with suggested gratuity of $70 per bag. Lodging at a Destination Kohler property is generally required to book the Straits Course in advance. 2026 pricing should be confirmed directly with Kohler. [View rates and book Whistling Straits](https://www.kohlerwisconsin.com/golf/golf-rates--safety-guidelines-and-policies?utm_campaign=the-most-underrated-golf-state-in-america&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=courses-newsletter-56d38d.beehiiv.com)


## WHERE TO STAY
### Build Around Your Anchor Course
![Erin Hills Cottage](https://res.cloudinary.com/dak67vton/image/upload/v1777010563/ec596eb6-c37c-4699-909f-3ed1a8f88920.png)
*Cottage at Erin Hills - (Erin Hills official Website)*

Each of these courses is its own destination and staying on property where available is always the best call.

Sand Valley and Erin Hills both have on-site lodging and stay-and-play packages that bundle everything into one booking. Whistling Straits requires a Kohler property stay to book the Straits Course in advance. The American Club at Kohler is world-class but you can also find nearby hotels and vacation rentals if you want to save on accommodation.

For a multi-course Wisconsin trip built around SentryWorld or Erin Hills, Airbnbs throughout the state give groups of any size a flexible and affordable base. Wisconsin is not a congested state. Driving between courses is easy and the scenery makes the drive part of the experience.


## FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
### What People Want to Know Before Booking
What People Want to Know About Wisconsin Golf

****Is Wisconsin a good golf destination?**** Yes, and it is one of the most underrated in the country. Wisconsin has multiple courses ranked in the top 100 public courses in America and several that rank in the top 50. The courses are world-class and the crowds have not yet caught up to the quality.

****When is the best time to golf in Wisconsin?**** May through early October. Late May and September offer the best combination of conditions, lighter crowds, and lower rates. Peak season runs June through August.


****How far is Sand Valley from Chicago?**** About three hours. It is one of the most accessible world-class golf resorts in the Midwest and makes for a very doable long weekend trip.

****Can you walk at all these courses?**** Erin Hills and Sand Valley are walking only. Whistling Straits Straits Course is also walking only. SentryWorld includes a cart in the green fee. If you love walking golf the way it was meant to be played, Wisconsin is the right destination.

****Do I need to stay on property to play Whistling Straits?**** For the Straits Course, lodging at a Destination Kohler property is generally required to secure a tee time in advance. Day guest availability exists but is limited and cannot be booked as far out. The Irish Course has more flexible booking options.

****How far in advance should I book Sand Valley?**** As far out as possible. Staying on property gives you priority access. For peak summer weekends, booking six to twelve months in advance is not unusual for the most sought-after tee times.

[social url="https://www.instagram.com/p/DPe4NNdgXho/" caption="Check out our video at Erin Hills" platform="ig"]


## THE BOTTOM LINE
### Go Before Everyone Else Does
Wisconsin is not on most golf bucket lists right now. That is the entire point.

SentryWorld and Erin Hills have already earned permanent spots on my all-time list. Sand Valley and Whistling Straits are two of the courses I am most excited to play anywhere in the world.

All of them are in the same state. A state most golfers are not talking about.

That gap between quality and awareness is exactly the definition of underrated. And it will not last forever.

See you next Thursday.

Beau
excerpt: I've played two of Wisconsin's best courses. Now I can't stop thinking about going back.
author: Beau Jordan
